content.problemStatement:
JJ has three integers AAA, BBB, and NNN. He can apply the following operation on AAA:


Select an integer XXX such that 1≤X<N1 \le X \lt N1≤X<N and set A:=A⊕XA := A \oplus XA:=A⊕X. (Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation.)
JJ wants to make AAA **equal** to BBB.

Determine the **minimum** number of operations required to do so. Print −1-1−1 if it is not possible.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains three integers AAA, BBB, and NNN — the parameters mentioned in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of operations required to make AAA equal to BBB. Output −1-1−1 if it is not possible to do so.
constraints: **Subtask 1 (30 points):** NNN is a power of 222.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: AAA is already equal to BBB. Hence we do not need to perform any operation.
Test Case 222: We can perform the operation with X=4X = 4X=4 which is <8\lt 8<8. Therefore A=3⊕4=7A = 3 \oplus 4 = 7A=3⊕4=7. Thus, only one operation is required.
Test Case 333: We can show that it is not possible to make AAA equal to BBB.

Explanation:
Note that the above sample case belongs to subtask 222.

Test Case 1: We can first perform the operation with X=1X = 1X=1 which is <3\lt 3<3. Therefore A=24⊕1=25A = 24 \oplus 1 = 25A=24⊕1=25. Then we can perform the operation with X=2X = 2X=2 which is <3\lt 3<3. Therefore A=25⊕2=27A = 25 \oplus 2 = 27A=25⊕2=27. Therefore we can make AAA equal to BBB in 222 operations. It can be shown that it is not possible to do this in less than 222 operations.
